canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, TREC-DL 2019 is consistently described as a benchmark dataset or test set from the Text REtrieval Conference (TREC) Deep Learning Track of 2019. Its prevailing use is for evaluating information retrieval systems, with papers frequently applying it to assess performance on tasks such as passage retrieval and document reranking. The benchmark is used to evaluate various systems, including "LLM-based ranking systems" ("HMoE: Heterogeneous Mixture of Experts for Language Modeling") and systems for "deep learning-based ad-hoc retrieval tasks" ("From perception to production..."). Some sources specify its composition, noting it is "derived from MS-MARCO-V1" ("LLMs Behind the Scenes...") and contains "human-annotated relevance judgments" ("From perception to production..."). A few papers provide more specific context, with one characterizing its use as "in-domain evaluation" ("LLMs Behind the Scenes...") and another describing it as a dataset that is "not reasoning intensive" ("SAFENUDGE...").
papers: Detecting Knowledge Boundary of Vision Large Language Models by Sampling-Based Inference
From perception to production: how acoustic invariance facilitates articulatory learning in a self-supervised vocal imitation model
HMoE: Heterogeneous Mixture of Experts for Language Modeling
LLMs Behind the Scenes: Enabling Narrative Scene Illustration
SAFENUDGE: Safeguarding Large Language Models in Real-time with Tunable Safety-Performance Trade-offs

definitions.text: A benchmark dataset from the TREC Deep Learning Track 2019, derived from MS-MARCO-V1, used for in-domain evaluation of information retrieval and reranking tasks.
definitions.paper: LLMs Behind the Scenes: Enabling Narrative Scene Illustration

definitions.text: A test set from the TREC Deep Learning Track 2019, used for evaluating passage retrieval systems, often in conjunction with MS MARCO.
definitions.paper: SAFENUDGE: Safeguarding Large Language Models in Real-time with Tunable Safety-Performance Trade-offs

definitions.text: Benchmark dataset from the Text REtrieval Conference used to evaluate LLM-based ranking systems on document relevance tasks, assessing performance in passage retrieval and reranking.
definitions.paper: HMoE: Heterogeneous Mixture of Experts for Language Modeling

definitions.text: Benchmark dataset for evaluating document re-ranking systems in information retrieval, focusing on deep learning-based ad-hoc retrieval tasks with human-annotated relevance judgments.
definitions.paper: From perception to production: how acoustic invariance facilitates articulatory learning in a self-supervised vocal imitation model
